query: value of anorectal endosonography in diagnosing dyssynergic defecation

retrieved_docs.title: Constipation  -- Evaluation -- Anorectal Manometry
retrieved_docs.content: Anorectal manometry evaluates motor and sensory anorectal function to identify potential causes of constipation or fecal incontinence. This test aids in the Hirschsprung disease diagnosis, but the gold standard test is a rectal suction biopsy.

retrieved_docs.title: Fecal Incontinence -- Evaluation
retrieved_docs.content: If incontinence is without diarrhea, more specific testing should be pursued. The most valuable tests for evaluating fecal incontinence are anorectal manometry and endoscopic ultrasound. Defecography is usually reserved for refractory symptoms or before operative planning intervention.

retrieved_docs.title: Constipation  -- Evaluation -- Defecography
retrieved_docs.content: This should be carried out if an obstruction is possible at the anal canal level. In this technique, the rectosigmoid is filled with barium paste, and the process of defecation is observed fluoroscopically. This test may demonstrate changes in the anorectal angle during defecation, transient rectal prolapse, intussusception, or pelvic floor weakness. [28]

retrieved_docs.title: Anoscopy -- Clinical Significance
retrieved_docs.content: While anoscopy appears to be the superior procedure for identifying distal anorectal pathologies [10] such as internal hemorrhoids, proctitis, or ulcerations (which cannot be reliably identified or differentiated from one another on a digital rectal exam) which may lead patients to present with complaints of bleeding or discomfort, there are significant limitations to this procedure. While anoscopy is highly sensitive for identifying pathology such as internal hemorrhoids, findings seen on anoscopy do not allow practitioners to rule out a proximal source of GI bleeding, which is identified subsequently on endoscopy up to 50% of the time. Therefore, the finding of a potential source of GI bleeding such as internal hemorrhoids in isolation does not allow the examiner to exclude an additional source of bleeding.

retrieved_docs.title: Pelvic Floor Dysfunction -- Evaluation
retrieved_docs.content: Defecography: the patient's rectum is filled with contrast. Images are obtained while the patient performs efforts for maintaining continence and evacuation while sitting on a special potty chair. Defecography is the "Gold Standard" for assessing pelvic floor disease. [33] Defecography provides assessment for rectal prolapse, rectocele, enterocele, perineal descent, and documentation of the anorectal angle with contraction and evacuation. Patients with dyssynergia defecation have a good correlation between abnormal EMG and balloon expulsion tests but do not match well with radiographic dyssynergia. [34]

retrieved_docs.title: Encopresis -- Evaluation
retrieved_docs.content: A barium enema should not be done in patients with mild constipation. It should be used in the assessment of the caliber of small or large intestines, which may provide beneficial clinical findings in the suspicion of  Hirschsprung disease or evaluation of anal atresia after the surgical intervention. [28]

retrieved_docs.title: Anoscopy -- Clinical Significance
retrieved_docs.content: Ultimately, anoscopy is a useful adjunct for screening for anorectal pathology which is inexpensive and can be accomplished quickly and without preparation. However, the procedure must not be unduly relied upon in the evaluation of a patient with rectal bleeding, since the presence of rectal pathology does not rule out a proximal source of bleeding.

retrieved_docs.title: Embryology, Rectum and Anal Canal -- Testing
retrieved_docs.content: Defecating proctography/Defecography : A study using X-ray imaging to evaluate anatomic defects of the anorectal region and function of the puborectalis muscle. A contrast filled paste gets initially introduced to the rectum, and the patient is instructed to defecate in a series of stages (relaxation, contraction, tensing of the abdomen, and evacuation). [16]

retrieved_docs.title: Physiology, Defecation -- Related Testing
retrieved_docs.content: Manometry can help assess colonic and anal sphincter contractility. The test is useful in identifying the cause of constipation or fecal incontinence. [8] Colonic transit time examination determines the speed at which stool moves through the colon. This test is performed by asking the patient to swallow a marker-filled capsule. Serial abdominal X-rays are taken until the marker capsule is completely expelled.

retrieved_docs.title: Rectal Exam -- Clinical Significance
retrieved_docs.content: Rectal examination findings include:

retrieved_docs.title: Encopresis -- Evaluation
retrieved_docs.content: Anorectal manometry can be used as a beneficial tool for patients with chronic constipation. Fundamentally, anorectal manometry assesses the anal pressure and rectal sensation. In clinical practice, sphincter muscles may need assessment when there is a spinal cord injury or local injuries in sphincter muscles. The internal anal sphincter may lose the ability to relax in a patient with Hirschsprung disease during the rectal balloon distention test. [27]

retrieved_docs.title: Constipation  -- Evaluation -- Anorectal Manometry
retrieved_docs.content: Colonic manometry consists of the colonoscopic insertion of a catheter throughout the length of the colon to measure segmental pressures. This diagnostic modality helps identify neurogenic and myogenic causes of constipation. However, there is no specific motility pattern that is diagnostic of idiopathic constipation.

retrieved_docs.title: Constipation  -- Evaluation
retrieved_docs.content: Routinely using any specific imaging or laboratory test in diagnosing pediatric functional constipation is not recommended. However, when symptoms fail to improve with a conventional medical approach, further diagnostic evaluation may clarify possible causes and help guide therapy. Such diagnostic tests include anorectal manometry, colonic manometry, colonic transit studies, and imaging such as x-ray and ultrasonography. [22] [23] [24] Regional colonic transit pattern does not conclusively identify evacuation disorders in constipated patients with delayed colonic transit. [25]

retrieved_docs.title: Anoscopy -- Introduction
retrieved_docs.content: Anoscopy is a bedside procedure that is inexpensive, can be performed quickly without bowel preparation or the need for sedation. With this method, the examiner can visualize the internal portions of the anal sphincter and the distal rectum. Prospective studies suggest that anoscopy has a higher sensitivity for anorectal lesions such as internal hemorrhoids, proctitis, lacerations, fistulas, ulcers, and masses than a flexible sigmoidoscopy. Up to 50% of rectal bleeding can be falsely attributed to hemorrhoids without internal examination to confirm this diagnosis. [1] [2] [3] [4]

retrieved_docs.title: Embryology, Rectum and Anal Canal -- Testing
retrieved_docs.content: Contrast enema : Used as one of the diagnostic methods for Hirschsprung disease. Useful for localization of the aganglionic segment by looking for a narrowed rectum. Diagnostic confirmation is via a rectal biopsy. [16]

retrieved_docs.title: Constipation  -- Evaluation
retrieved_docs.content: An extensive workup of the patient with constipation should be performed on an outpatient basis. Usually, the workup starts after 3 to 6 months of failed medical management. It is worthwhile to repeat rectal and perineal examinations at this point, even if it has been previously performed. Anorectal tests help distinguish defecatory disorders if over-the-counter options do not relieve constipation. [2] Laboratory investigations do not play a significant role in the initial evaluation of constipation.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 0 -- Level 3 -- Community 2573
retrieved_docs.content: Anal and rectal conditions are encompassed within the broader category of gastrointestinal disorder (اضْطِراب مَعِدِيّ مِعَوِيّ). As a subset of this wider gastrointestinal spectrum, anal and rectal conditions affect the distal portion of the digestive tract and share many pathophysiological mechanisms, diagnostic considerations, and therapeutic strategies with other gastrointestinal disorders. Recognizing that anal and rectal conditions have a broader relationship with gastrointestinal disorder encourages clinicians to adopt a comprehensive, system‑based approach to evaluation and management, ensuring that patient care addresses both localized anorectal pathology and its context within the entire gastrointestinal system.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 0 -- Level 3 -- Community 2649
retrieved_docs.content: Hemorrhoids is classified as a type of venous disorder (اضطراب الوريد), reflecting its origin in abnormal dilation and dysfunction of the venous vessels of the anorectal region. This relationship underscores that hemorrhoids arise from underlying vascular pathology, linking the clinical presentation of swollen, painful, or bleeding anal cushions directly to disturbances in venous blood flow and vessel integrity. Recognizing hemorrhoids as a manifestation of a broader venous disorder informs both diagnostic evaluation and therapeutic strategies, emphasizing the importance of addressing venous health to manage and prevent hemorrhoidal disease.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 17 -- Level 3 -- Community 2169
retrieved_docs.content: Hemorrhoids encompass presentations that are described as بواسير غير محددة من دون ذكر مضاعفات, which translates to unspecified hemorrhoids without mention of complications. This indicates that cases of hemorrhoids that lack detailed specification or documented complications are still classified within the broader condition of hemorrhoids, guiding clinicians to consider appropriate evaluation and management even when the presentation is nonspecific.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 10 -- Level 3 -- Community 2604
retrieved_docs.content: Hemorrhoids, also known by the Arabic term داء البواسير, is a medical condition affecting the anal and rectal region. Psyllium is identified as a therapeutic agent that may be used to treat داء البواسير, providing symptomatic relief through its fiber‑rich properties. In addition, a complete fiberoptic colonoscopy: hemorrhoids is recognized as a diagnostic procedure specifically employed to evaluate and confirm the presence and severity of داء البواسير, allowing clinicians to directly visualize the affected area and guide appropriate management.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 10 -- Level 4 -- Community 2882
retrieved_docs.content: Esophagoscopy (تنظير مريئي) is classified within the broader category of other gastrointestinal diagnostic procedures, indicating that it is one of several techniques used to evaluate the gastrointestinal tract and is part of a wider set of diagnostic tools for gastrointestinal assessment.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 15 -- Level 2 -- Community 771
retrieved_docs.content: Hemorrhoids (داء البواسير) are a common anorectal disorder that can be evaluated with a complete fiberoptic colonoscopy specifically directed at identifying hemorrhoidal disease. Dietary fiber supplementation with psyllium may be employed to treat hemorrhoids by softening stools and reducing straining. When conservative measures are insufficient, hemorrhoidectomy (استئصال البواسير) is a clinically associated surgical option for definitive management of hemorrhoids. This integrated view underscores the diagnostic role of colonoscopy, the therapeutic benefit of psyllium, and the surgical relationship of hemorrhoidectomy to hemorrhoids.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 0 -- Level 3 -- Community 1982
retrieved_docs.content: Constipation (إِمْساك) is clinically associated with vomiting (تَقَيُّء), indicating that patients who experience difficulty passing stool may also present with episodes of emesis, reflecting a possible shared gastrointestinal dysfunction or underlying condition that links these two symptoms.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 29 -- Level 1 -- Community 185
retrieved_docs.content: (treatment of rectal diverticulum) both exhibit a fistula morphology (فَتْك). Overall, the knowledge emphasizes precise identification of anatomical sites such as the vaginal wall and awareness of complications like fistula to guide effective surgical management of pelvic floor disorders and related diverticula.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 23 -- Level 3 -- Community 1737
retrieved_docs.content: The relationship has_pathology indicates that the therapeutic approach for علاج قيلة مستقيمية (treatment of rectal prolapse) is linked to the pathological condition فَتْق (fistula). Recognizing that treatment of rectal prolapse may be associated with or complicated by fistula formation is important for accurate diagnosis, appropriate management, and optimal patient care.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 2 -- Level 3 -- Community 1421
retrieved_docs.content: Enema is a medical intervention that is encompassed within the broader category of Enemas for Constipation, indicating that the procedure is part of the general approach to managing constipation through the use of enemas.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 21 -- Level 4 -- Community 2814
retrieved_docs.content: Diagnostic ultrasound of gastrointestinal tract is a diagnostic imaging procedure that is performed directly on the gastrointestinal system, enabling visualization of the organs and structures within the gastrointestinal tract for clinical assessment.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 14 -- Level 1 -- Community 200
retrieved_docs.content: a clinical correlation between constipation and diarrhea reflects alternating bowel‑motility disturbances that may accompany these cardiac pathologies, underscoring the need for integrated, multidisciplinary patient‑care strategies.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 10 -- Level 4 -- Community 2883
retrieved_docs.content: التنظير الداخلي للجهاز الهضمي and other bowel diagnostic procedures are both classified as subcategories of تنظير الجهاز الهضمي. This indicates that internal gastrointestinal endoscopy and additional bowel diagnostic procedures fall under the broader umbrella of gastrointestinal endoscopy, which encompasses various endoscopic techniques used to visualize, evaluate, and diagnose conditions of the gastrointestinal tract.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 18 -- Level 4 -- Community 2870
retrieved_docs.content: Colonoscopy and biopsy is classified as a component of إجراءات تصوير البطن والجهاز الهضمي (abdominal and gastrointestinal imaging procedures) and also as part of التنظير الداخلي للجهاز الهضمي (gastrointestinal endoscopic procedures). This dual categorization reflects that colonoscopy with biopsy involves both imaging of the abdominal and digestive tract and the use of endoscopic techniques to directly visualize the colon and obtain tissue samples for diagnostic evaluation. Recognizing colonoscopy and biopsy within these broader procedural groups helps clinicians understand its role in comprehensive gastrointestinal assessment and patient care.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 2 -- Level 3 -- Community 2680

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 19 -- Level 3 -- Community 1743
retrieved_docs.content: Surgical procedures on the colon and rectum are classified as a type of gastrointestinal tract surgery, representing a specific subset of abdominal surgical interventions.
